benchmarks.name: Authentication
benchmarks.value: N/A (embedded)

sections.narrative: Access control is TrustDB's strongest differentiator, backed by 99 CBAC unit tests, 92 RDKC tests, 21 EACB tests, 20 CBAC-RDKC integration tests, and 352 adversarial gym test functions across 28 files. CBAC implements a 6-state governance circuit breaker (Open, Restricted, Suspended, Quarantined, Sealed, EngagementAuthorized) with compare-and-swap semantics. REWG derives entitlements from graph position via bitwise AND — no ACL tables, no policy engine. EACB provides FROST threshold signatures for LAWS 3000.09 human-in-the-loop authorization. Access control is cryptographic: RDKC derives unique per-page keys from governance state. A CUI-clearance entity cannot derive the key for a SECRET page — verified by gym_g01 clearance boundary battery and gym_p01 pre-redaction leakage proof. Gap to 9.5: ML-DSA-87 post-quantum signatures (CNSA 2.0), FIPS ACVP formal validation, and timing side-channel proof on target hardware.

history.note: Updated test counts: 99 CBAC, 92 RDKC, 21 EACB, 352 gym functions across 28 files. 15,913 security LOC. 84 modules. Gap: ML-DSA-87, FIPS ACVP.

benchmarks.name: Encryption at Rest
benchmarks.value: AES-256-GCM-SIV per-page — 11 page_crypto tests, 92 RDKC tests

benchmarks.name: Per-Page Governance-Bound Keys
benchmarks.value: Unique key per page from 4 governance inputs — verified by gym_e02 (10 independence tests)

comparisons.value: Single key for entire database or collection — compromise one key, read everything

benchmarks.name: Structural Key Expiry
benchmarks.value: Keys underivable when governance changes — verified by gym_e01 (12 expiry tests)

comparisons.value: Manual key rotation or time-based expiry

benchmarks.value: ML-KEM-1024 + X25519 hybrid — 6 transport tests (CNSA 2.0 aligned)

benchmarks.value: 130 CAM tests — CNSA 2.0 profiles: HKDF-SHA384, AES-256-GCM-SIV, ML-KEM-1024

benchmarks.name: WAL Tamper Protection
benchmarks.value: HMAC-SHA384 chained format (PR #247) — 33 WAL tests total. Per-record WalProvenance. ACVP test vectors (RFC 4231)
benchmarks.source: PR #247. Chained HMAC: each tag = HMAC(key, prev_tag || provenance || data). WalProvenance: 26 bytes per record. CNSA 2.0 compliant.

benchmarks.name: FIPS 140-3 Validation
benchmarks.value: Not formally submitted — ACVP vectors built, algorithms implemented, boundary not yet formalized

sections.narrative: Encryption is TrustDB's defining capability. 125,181 total LOC across 527 source files in 84 modules. Security-specific: 15,913 LOC across 14 security modules (rdkc, cam, cbac, eacb, tee, zk, fhe, hsm, tpm, puf, vdf, ldp, oram, wal) with 451 unit tests + 352 adversarial gym functions. Every 16KB page encrypted with AES-256-GCM-SIV using a unique key derived by RDKC from 4 governance inputs. ML-KEM-1024 + X25519 hybrid post-quantum transport. TEE/HSM/TPM/PUF hardware trust chain. LDP + ORAM + FHE privacy layers. ZK-STARK governance proofs. WAF=3.03 invariance (zero governance overhead). WAL HMAC-SHA384 chained with per-record provenance. Gap to 9.5: FIPS 140-3 formal submission (implementation ready, certification process not started).

history.note: Updated: 125K LOC, 527 files, 84 modules. Security: 15,913 LOC, 451 unit tests, 352 gym functions. 14 security modules. Gap: FIPS 140-3 formal submission.

benchmarks.name: WAL HMAC Chain (Tamper-Proof Audit)
benchmarks.value: HMAC-SHA384 chained format (PR #247) — 33 WAL tests total. Per-record WalProvenance (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp). ACVP vectors (RFC 4231)
benchmarks.source: PR #247. Chained HMAC: each tag = HMAC(key, prev_tag || provenance || data). WalProvenance: 26 bytes per record (NIST AU-10 non-repudiation). Static or RDKC-derived key modes. CNSA 2.0 compliant.

benchmarks.name: SSP Evidence Appendix (NIST SP 800-53)
benchmarks.value: GYM-R01 — automated control mapping from 28 gym files to 9 NIST control families
benchmarks.source: GYM-R01 on main. Maps tests to AC-3, AU-2, IA-3, SC-8, SC-12, SC-13, SC-28, SI-7, SI-16.

benchmarks.name: Compliance Export (SOX/HIPAA/NIST)
benchmarks.value: ComplianceReport with 7 control attestations (PR #250). SOX 404, HIPAA 164.312(b)/(c)(1), NIST AU-2/3/10/12

benchmarks.name: SIEM Integration (CEF/STIX)
benchmarks.value: CEF for ArcSight/Splunk + STIX 2.1 for Sentinel/Elastic (PR #250). 4 event types.

sections.narrative: Auditing is the most complete pillar at 9.5. ProvenanceChain tracks who/what/when on every entity at zero overhead. TCI discovers implicit relationships at flush time. CBAC audit trail records every state change with CAS-versioned immutability. WAL HMAC-SHA384 chained format (PR #247) provides tamper-proof audit with per-record provenance (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp) — 26 bytes overhead, 33 tests, ACVP vectors for NIST validation prep. Compliance export (PR #250) covers SOX 404, HIPAA, NIST AU controls. SIEM integration outputs CEF for Splunk/ArcSight and STIX 2.1 for Sentinel/Elastic. GYM-R01 SSP Evidence maps 28 gym files to 9 NIST SP 800-53 control families. The only gap to 10.0: FIPS 140-3 lab certification of the HMAC implementation (process, not engineering).

history.note: Updated test counts. WAL HMAC chain (PR #247), compliance export + SIEM (PR #250), GYM-R01 SSP evidence all on main.
